memoryArtifacts.headline: John I, Lord of Egmond (before 1310-28 December 1369)
memoryArtifacts.description:
John I, Lord of Egmond (before 1310-28 December 1369) was Lord of Egmond, Lord of IJsselstein, bailiff of Kennemerland (1353-1354) and stadtholder of Holland.

«b»Life«/b»
He was a son of Walter II an …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Jan I van Egmond於1310年之前出生在荷蘭努-拉藍德艾姆-賓時，他的父親Wouter II van Egmond26歲，母親Beatrijs van Doortoge20歲。他於1330年5月20日與Guyotte van Amstel IJsselstein在荷蘭武特雷特易瑟爾斯坦結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和8個女兒的父母。他去世於1369年12月28日在荷蘭武特雷特易瑟爾斯坦，埋葬在荷蘭武特雷特易瑟爾斯坦。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jan I van Egmond于1310年之前出生在荷兰努-拉藍德艾姆-賓时，他的父亲Wouter II van Egmond26岁，母亲Beatrijs van Doortoge20岁。他于1330年5月20日与Guyotte van Amstel IJsselstein在荷兰武特雷特易瑟爾斯坦结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和8个女儿的父母。他去世于1369年12月28日在荷兰武特雷特易瑟爾斯坦，埋葬在荷兰武特雷特易瑟爾斯坦。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Egmond
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Esmond (Old English Ēastmund, ēast ‘grace, beauty’ + mund ‘protection’). A Norman French cognate, of ancient Germanic origin, is also found. See also Eastman .

variants.name: Eastman
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Estmund (Old English Ēastmund, from ēast ‘east’ + mund ‘protection’). See also Esmond .
English: occasionally a variant of Heasman, a topographic name for a dweller ‘(in the) brushwood’, from Old English hǣse ‘brushwood’ + mann (compare Hayes 3).
Americanized form (translation into English) of Swedish Östman (see Ostman ) and North German Ostmann or Östmann (see Oestmann ).

nameMeanings.name: Jan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

en.lifeSummary: When Jan I van Egmond was born before 1310, in Egmond-Binnen, North Holland, Netherlands, his father, Wouter II van Egmond, was 26 and his mother, Beatrijs van Doortoge, was 20. He married Guyotte van Amstel IJsselstein on 20 May 1330, in IJsselstein, Utrecht, Netherlands. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 8 daughters. He died on 28 December 1369, in IJsselstein, Utrecht, Netherlands, and was buried in IJsselstein, Utrecht, Netherlands.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jan I van Egmond est né avant 1310, à Egmond-Binnen, Hollande-septentrionale, Pays-Bas, son père, Wouter II van Egmond, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Beatrijs van Doortoge, avait 20 ans. Il a épousé Guyotte van Amstel IJsselstein le 20 mai 1330, à IJsselstein, Utrecht, Pays-Bas. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 8 filles. Il est décédé le 28 décembre 1369, à IJsselstein, Utrecht, Pays-Bas, et a été enterré à IJsselstein, Utrecht, Pays-Bas.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jan I van Egmond vor 1310 in Egmond-Binnen, Nord-Holland, Niederlande geboren wurde, war sein Vater Wouter II van Egmond 26 und seine Mutter Beatrijs van Doortoge 20. Er hat am 20. Mai 1330 in IJsselstein, Utrecht, Niederlande Guyotte van Amstel IJsselstein geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 8 Töchter. Er starb am 28. Dezember 1369 in IJsselstein, Utrecht, Niederlande und wurde in IJsselstein, Utrecht, Niederlande begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jan I van Egmond è nato il prima del 1310 nel Egmond-Binnen, Noord-Holland, Paesi Bassi, il suo padre, Wouter II van Egmond, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Beatrijs van Doortoge, aveva 20 anni. Lui sposato Guyotte van Amstel IJsselstein il 20 maggio 1330, nel IJsselstein, Utrecht, Paesi Bassi. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 8 figlie. Morì il 28 dicembre 1369, nel IJsselstein, Utrecht, Paesi Bassi, e fu sepolto a IJsselstein, Utrecht, Paesi Bassi.

ja.lifeSummary: Jan I van Egmondが1310年以前にオランダノオルド-hオランドエグモンド-ビンで生まれた時，父Wouter II van Egmondは26歳，母Beatrijs van Doortogeは20歳であった。1330年5月20日にオランダウトレチトイジセルステインでGuyotte van Amstel IJsselsteinと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と8人の娘の親であった。彼は1369年12月28日にオランダウトレチトイジセルステインで死去し，オランダウトレチトイジセルステインにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Jan I van Egmond(이)가 1310년 이전 네덜란드 노르드-홀랜드 그몽-빈에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Wouter II van Egmond(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Beatrijs van Doortoge(은)는 20세였다. 그는 1330년 5월 20일 네덜란드 우트레ch트 이j셀스타인에서 Guyotte van Amstel IJsselstein(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 여덟 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1369년 12월 28일 네덜란드 우트레ch트 이j셀스타인에서 사망했으며 네덜란드 우트레ch트 이j셀스타인에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jan I van Egmond nasceu antes 1310, em Egmond-Binnen, Holanda do Norte, Países Baixos, seu pai, Wouter II van Egmond, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Beatrijs van Doortoge, tinha 20 anos. Ele casou-se com Guyotte van Amstel IJsselstein em 20 de maio de 1330, em IJsselstein, Utrecht, Países Baixos. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 8 filhas. Ele faleceu em 28 de dezembro de 1369, em IJsselstein, Utrecht, Países Baixos, e foi sepultado em IJsselstein, Utrecht, Países Baixos.

ro.Slot O/D Hoef, Noord-Holland, Netherland: De Hoef, Noord-Holland, Ţările de Jos

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jan I van Egmond родился перед 1310 г. в местности эгмонд-биннен, нурд-холланд, Нидерланды, его отцу по имени Wouter II van Egmond было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Beatrijs van Doortoge, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Guyotte van Amstel IJsselstein 20 мая 1330 г. в местности иджсселстин, утречт, Нидерланды. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 8 дочерей. Он умер 28 декабря 1369 г. в местности иджсселстин, утречт, Нидерланды и был похоронен в местности иджсселстин, утречт, Нидерланды.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jan I van Egmond nació antes de 1310, en Egmond-Binnen, Noord-Holland, Países Bajos, su papá, Wouter II van Egmond, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Beatrijs van Doortoge, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Guyotte van Amstel IJsselstein el 20 de mayo de 1330, en IJsselstein, Utrecht, Países Bajos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 8 hijas. Murió el 28 de diciembre de 1369, en IJsselstein, Utrecht, Países Bajos, y fue sepultado en IJsselstein, Utrecht, Países Bajos.
